result.summary: Mailchimp exposes two documented HTTP APIs for developers—the Marketing API and Transactional API—and a cataloged streamable HTTP MCP server endpoint for Intuit Mailchimp.
result.description: Mailchimp is Intuit's email marketing and customer engagement platform. It provides tools for managing audiences, campaigns, automations, and transactional messaging.

marketing_api_key.label: Mailchimp Marketing API key
marketing_api_key.setup: Sign in to your Mailchimp account, open the [API Keys section](https://us1.admin.mailchimp.com/account/api/), click **Create New Key**, name it, and copy the generated key immediately. The key grants full account access and is shown only when created; Mailchimp's quick start and fundamentals docs say it is required for direct access to your own account's Marketing API data.

marketing_oauth_app.label: Mailchimp OAuth 2 app (client ID/client secret)
marketing_oauth_app.setup: To access other users' Mailchimp Marketing data, register an app from the [Registered Apps page](https://us1.admin.mailchimp.com/account/oauth2/), click [Register An App](https://us19.admin.mailchimp.com/account/oauth2/client/), complete the form, and save the `client_id` and `client_secret`. Then run the OAuth 2 authorization-code flow described in the [OAuth guide](https://mailchimp.com/developer/marketing/guides/access-user-data-oauth-2/) to obtain a bearer access token. The guide states Mailchimp Marketing access tokens do not expire unless revoked.

transactional_api_key.label: Mailchimp Transactional API key
transactional_api_key.setup: Create a Mailchimp Transactional account, then open the [SMTP & API Info](https://mandrillapp.com/settings/index) page to view your credentials. Mailchimp's SMTP docs state you can use any active API key for your account as your SMTP password, and the same key is used for the Transactional API.

variables.name: dc
variables.resolveFrom: Mailchimp account data center/server prefix; docs say it is the first part of the account URL or suffix of the API key, and OAuth metadata can also provide it.
variables.description: Mailchimp data center subdomain such as `us6` or `us19`.
